title: Edusave Account
text:
Edusave Account - SupportGoWhere
Edusave Account
Share link
Provides $230 to $290 annually for approved fees and programmes for every Singapore Citizen child, aged 7 to 16 or studying in a primary or secondary school. Ad-hoc top-ups may be made by the Government to the child’s Edusave account periodically.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Receive annual contributions of $230 to $290 for all eligible Singapore Citizen children
No application needed
Collapse all sections
About this support

Annual contributions to Edusave accounts for primary school students: $230

Annual contributions to Edusave accounts for secondary school students: $290

Funds can be used for approved school enrichment programs, miscellaneous fees, approved educational activities and personal learning devices / assistive devices.

Edusave account balances earn interest at a rate pegged to the CPF Ordinary Account (CPF-OA), currently at 2.5%

Who is this for?

You must be a Singapore Citizen child, either aged 7 to 16 years old or enrolled in a Ministry of Education (MOE)-funded primary / secondary school

Junior college and Millennia Institute students do not receive annual contributions but can use remaining Edusave funds

How to apply?

No application required.

Where can I find help?

View more details

MOE Hotline: 6872 2220

Submit an enquiry

MOE Customer Service Centre 1 North Buona Vista Drive MOE Building, Podium Block Singapore 138675

Note: By appointment only.

Scheme last updated 02 Mar 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
